content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- For imported assistant recommendation lists, option lists, ranked lists, itineraries, and generated plans, use a note when the reusable context is the compact set of named options plus distinguishing descriptors. Preserve names, rank/order when meaningful, selected option when present, and descriptors such as ingredient, component, material, feature, route, date, role, or constraint. Do not collapse the note to only the option the user chose.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory write model uses Direct structured path, Note path, Consolidation, and Repair; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, the core write model distinguishes two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths: `Direct structured path` is for safely normalized facts, stable preferences, commitments/tasks, and status changes; `Note path` is for important but not-yet-hard-fact material such as design direction, rationale, evolving plans, lessons, document digests, and contextual framing; `Consolidation` promotes mature notes when signal is strong enough; `Repair` fixes memory degradation such as duplicates, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура'
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> generated_artifact_detail; Repair fixes memory degradation such as duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.; scope=Repair in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory architecture uses four axes; In `v3`, memory is modeled along four axes: **Representation** (`Source`, `Claim`, `Note`, `Profile`, `Task`, `Episode`), **Lifecycle** (write, reconcile, consolidate, repair, retain/archive/delete), **Retrieval policy** (`claim-first`, `note-first`, `mixed`, `evidence-first`, bounded budgets), and **Scope and lineage** (`scope_json`, `scope_text`, source refs, origin note, memory runs). These axes are meant to prevent memory from degrading into either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> retrieves_before_extraction; In the v3 memory architecture write path, candidate memories are retrieved before extraction, including similar claims, similar notes, open tasks, the existing profile, candidate entities or aliases, and recent evidence.; scope=Applies to the write path described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory write path steps; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, section 11 defines the `v3` write path as an ordered flow: persist raw source, route to `noop` / `direct_structured_write` / `note_write` / `mixed`, retrieve candidate memories, canonicalize entities, run specialized extraction prompts, reconcile candidates with existing items, apply all updates in one transaction, and schedule maintenance such as consolidation, duplicate merge, stale cleanup, profile repair, alias merge, and retention/archive.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path'
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> applies_memory_updates_atomically; In the v3 memory architecture write path, memory transaction application atomically upserts entities, applies claim, note, and task operations, updates the profile projection, and stores the memory run log.; scope=Applies to the write path described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11.
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Memory update must reconcile rather than append-only; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, section 9 states that memory writes must decide whether to write, retrieve similar or conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply a policy such as `insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, or `noop`. Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 9 'Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only'
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory read path retrieval modes and budgets; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, section 12 defines the read path: decide if memory is needed, load core blocks (`short profile`, `active tasks`, optional session summary), choose a retrieval mode (`Claim-first`, `Note-first`, `Mixed`, or `Evidence-first / evidence-fallback`), enforce explicit `v3` budget caps, then compose an answer that distinguishes verified vs provisional information, handles conflicts, respects scope boundaries, can abstain, and does not present notes as hard facts without qualification.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 12. Read path'
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> has_constraint; In the memory architecture, retrieval/reconciliation must not be skipped during updates because the database would quickly accumulate duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam.; scope=Applies to the memory architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 9.
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> schedules_memory_maintenance; In the v3 memory architecture write path, memory maintenance is scheduled by debounce or cron for note consolidation, duplicate merge, stale cleanup, profile repair, alias merge, and retention/archive.; scope=Applies to the write path described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 11.
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended v3 memory architecture pipeline; The recommended architecture routes `User / Tool / Document` input through `Source Log`, `Memory Router`, `Write-Time Retrieval`, and `Entity Canonicalizer`; then specialized writers (`Claim Extractor`, `Task Updater`, `Profile Updater`, `Note Constructor`) reconcile into `Postgres + pgvector`, which is maintained by `Background Consolidator`, `Memory Repair Worker`, and `Retention / Forgetting Worker`. At read time, `Assistant Runtime` uses `Read-Time Retrieval Planner` to query storage and feed `Answer Composer`, which returns to the runtime.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура'
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> responsible_for_work; In the memory architecture, the Memory worker / manager is responsible for routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention.; scope=Applies to the division of responsibilities in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 10.2.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> has_constraint; In the memory architecture, both a Claim and a Note should be written when one material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7, combined Claim and Note writing criteria
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_constraint; In the memory architecture, update must not be append-only; at write time the system must decide whether to write memory, find similar or conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply an insert/update/supersede/retract/noop policy.; scope=Applies to the memory architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 9.
- note hot-path:note:<6> DOC_DIGEST; Truth state and retrieval state must stay separate; In `agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md`, section 8 states that `Truth state` answers whether an item is current as knowledge, while `Retrieval state` answers how it should be searched and kept in the hot path. They can diverge: a note may be `resolved` but still `warm` for rationale retrieval, and a claim may be `superseded` but still `warm` as historical evidence.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 8 'Truth state и retrieval state'
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_constraint; In the memory architecture, truth state and retrieval state are separate concepts and must not be mixed.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 8, truth state and retrieval state separation
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> generated_artifact_detail; Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile entries, or episodes when the signal has become strong enough.; scope=Consolidation in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_constraint; In the memory architecture, a Note should be written when information is already important and useful for future retrieval but too context-rich or too early to become a hard fact.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7, Note writing criteria

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Note; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=Note; reason=Target discusses the memory representation `Note`, which safely matches the existing Note concept.
- mention=Note maturity; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Note maturity; reason=The section introduces note maturity as a reusable concept and no existing candidate safely matches it.
- mention=Memory repair; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=CONCEPT; name=Memory repair; reason=The target discusses Repair as a background workflow; existing Memory Repair Worker may be related but is not a safe identity match.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=01. Architecture|agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md|01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=01. Architecture agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair MARKDOWN Document title: 01. Architecture Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair Document lines: 467-508 ## 13. Note maturity Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости. Полезный паттерн: - `fresh` - `stabilizing` - `mature` - `consolidated` Что может повышать maturity: - note использовался в retrieval - note подтвержден несколькими sources - note не конфликтует с новыми данными - note пережил какое-то время без supersede - note важен для recurring rationale questions Что может понижать или закрывать note: - явный supersede - устаревший scope - отсутствие использования - слабая поддержка evidence ## 14. Memory repair Repair — это отдельный background workflow. Что он чинит: - duplicate notes / claims - contradictory active items - profile drift - stale task states - bad entity merges - note spam - budget leakage в retrieval Почему это надо выделять отдельно: - consolidation делает память богаче - repair делает память здоровее Это разные работы.
        predicate_hints=

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:3ba1fe9fde8041a82c660a92539e97ff] EXTERNAL
Source metadata: source_kind=document; origin=pasted_document_section; document_type=MARKDOWN; source_ref=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; title=01. Architecture; heading=01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair; imported_at=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.
